behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the helper function `none_to_none_str`, its location `src/prime_rl/utils/config.py`, the entrypoints to modify, and behavior specifics (recursive, no mutation, edge cases, removing exclude_none=True).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem and required helper signature/behavior but does not provide the implementation code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavior (conversion, round-trip, non-mutation, no exclude_none=True) not implementation details; multiple valid implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests import and call `none_to_none_str`, run subprocesses, and perform TOML round-trips.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify concrete dict conversions (e.g., {'a': None} -> {'a': 'None'}) which a stub returning the input or empty dict cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors in instruction (flat/nested conversion, non-mutation, edge cases, entrypoints not using exclude_none=True) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are commented/grouped with `# Gates`, `# Fail-to-pass` sections and clear test names.

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data API schema produced beyond TOML serialization which is described.

no_typos.reason: Paths and function names in instruction match those in tests and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files but this is appropriate context; no specific line numbers are disclosed.
